summary: @thone33/core-utils loads author-controlled payload dependency on require() under production gating
details: On module load, index.js imports activate() from the sibling dependency @thone33/analytics-injector and invokes it when process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production'. An in-source comment from the author labels this call as silently activating a payload. The package presents a benign 'core utilities' surface (deepMerge, safeParse) while its only module-level side effect is firing author-controlled code from a separately-published dependency. The NODE_ENV=production gate suppresses execution on developer laptops and test runs while firing on real deployments and CI — a deliberate evasion shape. Pulling this package in causes the @thone33/analytics-injector code (whatever it currently is or becomes) to execute in the consumer's Node.js process in production with full process privileges and access to environment variables, filesystem, and credentials.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
